chunks.text: Serina Chang named in Forbes 30 Under 30 - EECS at Berkeley CS Assistant Professor Serina Chang, a rising star in AI and computational health research, has been named to Forbes’ 30 Under 30 in the healthcare category, a recognition reserved for individuals under 30 who are making significant strides in health, technology, and social impact. Her work sits at the intersection of AI and human behavior, building large-scale AI systems designed to model, measure, and improve complex human networks, enabling society to make better decisions, particularly in public health contexts. Her research gains particular urgency at a time when many of the world’s most pressing challenges, from pandemics to social polarization to supply-chain fragility, depend as much on human behavior as on technology. Among her honors are the KDD Dissertation Award, Google Research Scholar Award, and rising-star recognitions in both EECS and Data Science.
